stocks.name: Enbridge Inc.
stocks.thesis: Record 2025: GAAP earnings $7.1B ($3.23/share, +38% YoY), adjusted EBITDA $20B (+7%), DCF $12.5B (+4%). 31st consecutive dividend increase to $3.88/share (+3%). $39B secured backlog (+35% since Enbridge Day), $14B new projects sanctioned in 2025 including Mainline Optimization, Eiger Express expansion to 3.7 Bcf/d, and renewable power deals with Meta. 20th consecutive year of achieving/exceeding guidance. RBC target raised to $76. Near ATH but valuation justified — add on any pullback to $65-67.
stocks.risks: D/EBITDA at 4.8x — manageable but limited buffer. Ongoing equity dilution from $39B growth capex. Trading near ATH ($74.39) with limited margin of safety at current levels. Best re-entry $65-67 CAD for 5.8-6.0% yield.

stocks.name: Bank of Nova Scotia
stocks.thesis: Q1 2026 earnings released Feb 24: $2.3B net income vs $993M year ago (+132% YoY) — massive beat. Pacific Alliance diversification (LatAm) is contrarian value play. At ~$104 CAD, trading above 200-day MA with fresh earnings catalyst. Add on pullback to $98–101 CAD for yield ~4.4–4.5%.
stocks.risks: Higher international credit exposure (LatAm) vs RY/TD creates more earnings volatility across credit cycles. Not a core sleep-at-night holding at full weight.

stocks.name: TELUS Corporation
stocks.thesis: 5G capex tapering driving FCF surge — record $2.2B FCF in 2025, up 11% YoY with 10%+ CAGR guided through 2028. Committed to 3-8% annual dividend increases from 2026 through 2028. A 9% yield with dividend growth in a telecom with hard infrastructure moat.
stocks.risks: High debt load; TELUS International drag and CRTC regulatory risk remain. Dividend growth lower end of 3-8% range if macro deteriorates.

stocks.name: Allied Properties REIT
stocks.thesis: Office REIT trading near 52-week lows, down 57% from 52-week high. Dividend was cut from C$0.15/month to C$0.06/month but forward yield still ~7.5% at current price. Analyst consensus target C$14.33 implies +48% upside. Distressed but not dead — high-quality urban office assets in major Canadian cities.
stocks.risks: DISTRESSED. Negative EPS (-$4.10). Office sector under structural WFH pressure. Dividend was already cut once — another cut possible. Not suitable as a core dividend holding — speculative recovery play only.

stocks.name: Canadian Natural Resources
stocks.thesis: Canada's largest oil sands producer with 24 consecutive years of dividend growth. $6.3B 2026 capex budget targeting high-return oil sands growth. Long-life, low-decline assets mean dividends are structurally sustainable across oil price cycles. Analyst avg target implies ~14% upside.
stocks.risks: Oil price exposure — dividend safety depends on WTI staying above ~$50. Tariff/trade war impacts on Canadian energy exports. Not for the risk-averse.

stocks.name: ARC Resources
stocks.thesis: Q4 2025: Record production 408K BOE/day (+7% YoY), Kakwa a machine at 215K BOE/day with record condensate. 11% dividend increase. Commenced LNG Canada deliveries to Shell — first international shipments via US Gulf Coast within ~1 year. Realized nat gas C$3.77/Mcf (C$1.50 above AECO). BUT: Attachie Upper Montney wells underperforming — casing deformation issues, development pace slowed. Stock sold off 10% on earnings but has recovered. Desjardins raised target to C$34.50, Raymond James downgraded to Market Perform at $28. Consensus ~C$32.
stocks.risks: Attachie execution risk — Upper Montney wells not meeting expectations, casing deformation issues could delay the asset's full ramp. Nat gas commodity exposure. Raymond James downgrade signals some analyst caution. Trade war/tariff risk on Canadian energy exports.

stocks.name: Cogeco Communications
stocks.thesis: Deep value telecom: 9.4x P/E vs sector 15x, P/B below 1.0. 21-year consecutive dividend growth streak with 7% recent raise and 5.8% yield. Q1 FY2026 in line with plan — US operations (Breezeline) improving. Q2 results April 9. Expanding into wireless as MVNO. S&P and Moody's improved credit outlooks Jan 2026, Morningstar 5-star rated. Consensus target C$76-80, TipRanks avg C$89. Income play with turnaround upside — well-covered dividend limits downside.
stocks.risks: Revenue declining ~4% YoY — cord-cutting structural headwind. $4.64B debt at 3.24x EBITDA. National Bank cut target to C$66 (below current price). Competitive pressure from Big 3 telcos in Canada and cable overbuilders in US. Currently underwater from your buy.

stocks.name: Enghouse Systems
stocks.thesis: Canadian B2B software acquirer down 48% from 52-week high. $269M cash, zero debt. Serial acquisition model (41 deals since 1984). Forward P/E 10.2x vs software sector 20-30x. All three buy-low signals fire. Next earnings March 5.
stocks.risks: 89% payout ratio — dividend at risk if earnings decline further. Revenue flat/declining. Technically in downtrend.

stocks.name: Alaris Equity Partners
stocks.thesis: Royalty income trust generating stable cash flows from private mid-market businesses, with an 8.82% dividend raise in January 2026. Crossed the 200-day moving average signalling improving momentum.
stocks.risks: Currently trading ABOVE all historical averages — not a screaming value; better entry would be around $20.

stocks.name: RioCan REIT
stocks.thesis: Grocery-anchored retail REIT trading at 12x forward FFO vs 15x long-term historical average — a 20% valuation discount implying ~$24.40 fair value (25% upside). Three analyst target upgrades in four days from National Bank, CIBC, and BMO; 19M unit buyback underway.
stocks.risks: Rate-sensitive retail REIT; Canadian consumer spending slowdown and historical dividend cut in COVID create psychological overhang.

stocks.name: BCE Inc.
stocks.thesis: ⚠️ DIVIDEND CUT — BCE announced a cut from $3.99 to $1.75/share (56% reduction) alongside restructuring and job reductions. The investment thesis as a dividend compounder is broken until management demonstrates FCF recovery.
stocks.risks: Dividend cut is structural, not tactical. Avoid until there is evidence of FCF stabilization and a credible path back to dividend growth.

stocks.name: Fortis Inc.
stocks.thesis: 52 consecutive years of dividend increases — the longest streak in Canada. $28.8B capex plan drives 7% annual rate base growth through 2030, supporting 4-6% dividend growth guidance. Rate-cut tailwind has re-rated the stock from 2024 lows (~$61) to current ATH (~$78); at P/E ~16x and 3.2% yield the stock is fairly valued, but 4% above average analyst consensus. Wait for a dip to $68-72 CAD (3.5-3.7% yield) for ideal entry.
stocks.risks: No margin of safety at ATH — any BoC rate pause or reversal hits utilities hard and fast; stock is 4% above average analyst target of $74.97 CAD.

stocks.name: Shopify Inc.
stocks.thesis: Canadian tech flagship. Down 22% in 6 weeks from $168.45 (Jan 6 high) — macro/tariff fears creating potential entry. Analyst consensus Buy, avg target ~$162 USD (~$232 CAD) = 77% upside from current. Commerce infrastructure story intact: Payments, Capital, Markets, B2B growing. No dividend — pure growth play. Best entry zone $115-125 CAD if it pulls back further.
stocks.risks: No dividend, elevated valuation even post-selloff (fwd P/E still 50x+). Revenue growth decelerating from hypergrowth era. Macro sensitivity. Tariff/trade war rhetoric hurts e-commerce sentiment.

stocks.name: Brookfield Asset Management Ltd.
stocks.thesis: World-class alternative asset manager with $1T+ AUM across infra, renewable energy, real estate, private equity, credit. Record 2025 results announced Feb 27. 15% dividend increase announced same day. Analyst consensus target ~$63.94 USD (~$91 CAD) = 27% upside from current CAD price. Fee-related earnings growing 20%+ annually. Long-duration compounding machine with a growing income stream. Best alternative asset manager on TSX.
stocks.risks: Payout ratio 115%+ in USD terms — dividend covered by fee-related earnings but optics are weak. Rate sensitivity (AUM growth slows when rate environment hurts fundraising). Complex structure — BAM vs BN (Brookfield Corp) can be confusing.

stocks.name: iShares S&P/TSX Composite High Dividend Index ETF
stocks.thesis: iShares high-dividend Canadian equity ETF — 75 holdings across energy, financials, utilities, REITs. Monthly distributions ($0.112/month = ~$1.34/yr). Ex-div Feb 24, 2026. Low-cost passive income — simple, diversified Canadian dividend exposure in one ticker. Good core income holding for TFSA/RRSP. Near 52wk high so not a screaming entry — add on dips below $34.
stocks.risks: Near 52wk high — limited short-term upside. Heavy energy and financials weighting = sector concentration. Passive instrument won't avoid dividend cuts within holdings (e.g. BCE-type situations). MER small but not zero.

stocks.name: iShares Core Growth ETF Portfolio
stocks.thesis: One-ticker diversified growth portfolio — 80% global equities / 20% bonds. MER 0.17% (just reduced from 0.18% Dec 2025). Holds iShares equity and bond ETFs across global markets. Simple, low-cost, automatic rebalancing. Ideal for TFSA growth allocation — set-and-forget core holding. Not a dividend story — total return play.
stocks.risks: Not a dividend vehicle (small distributions). 20% bond allocation drags return vs pure equity in bull markets. Currency exposure to USD/global. Near 52wk high.

stocks.name: Advanced Micro Devices CDR (CAD Hedged)
stocks.thesis: CDR (Canadian Depositary Receipt) of AMD — CAD-hedged exposure to Advanced Micro Devices without USD currency risk. AMD US is a top AI/data centre chip play alongside NVIDIA. MI300X GPU gaining traction in hyperscaler AI workloads. Forward P/E ~29x (reasonable for AI semis). CDR ratio means each unit represents a fraction of one AMD share. Analyst consensus targets imply significant upside on AI infrastructure build-out. NOTE: Verify current CDR price — approximate entry used here.
stocks.risks: No dividend. High volatility (AI hype cycle). NVIDIA dominates GPU market — AMD is the #2 play. CDR liquidity lower than NASDAQ AMD. AI capex could compress if hyperscaler spending slows. Price is approximate — verify before buying.

stocks.name: Tourmaline Oil Corp.
stocks.thesis: Canada's largest natural gas producer. Base dividend + special dividends = ~$3.55/yr (~5.6% at current price). Monthly distributions. Real tailwinds: LNG Canada coming online, rising US nat gas demand, AI data centre electricity demand. Just crossed its 200-day MA ($62.67) — improving momentum. Earnings Mar 11, 2026. Best entry below $58-60 CAD for a 6%+ yield and meaningful upside. Nat gas is the clean energy bridge fuel — TOU is the dominant Canadian play.
stocks.risks: Special dividends variable — base dividend alone is ~2.5%, so yield depends on commodity prices and cash flow. Natural gas price is cyclical. If nat gas prices drop, specials get cut first. Current price near short-term resistance.

stocks.name: Canadian Utilities Limited
stocks.thesis: Canada's longest dividend streak — 54 consecutive annual increases since 1972. The $2.9B Yellowhead Pipeline (100% contracted, AUC-approved, construction 2026) and CETO project are rate-base growth catalysts that drove re-rating from $33.47 to $47. BoC rate cuts provide structural tailwind for regulated utilities. At $42-44, yield opens to 4.3-4.4% with 15-20% total return potential to $50-54 fair value.
stocks.risks: Currently 8% above analyst consensus of $43.43 — post-earnings momentum may be short-term overextended. AUC regulatory risk on allowed returns (2025 temp ROE cut already proved this). $2.9B+ capex financed with debt; rate reversal compresses returns.

stocks.name: Waste Connections Inc.
stocks.thesis: Canada's largest waste management company — essential, recession-proof, irreplaceable infrastructure. Nobody stops throwing out garbage in a downturn. 12+ years of double-digit dividend growth. Analyst consensus Buy, avg target ~$205 USD. Pure quality compounder — total return story, not a yield story. Buy on broad market pullbacks.
stocks.risks: Low current yield (0.75%) — not for income investors. Premium valuation requires continued revenue growth. US market concentration.

stocks.name: Granite Real Estate Investment Trust
stocks.thesis: Premium industrial/logistics REIT with warehouses and distribution centres across Canada, US, and Europe. Monthly distributions (~3.87% yield). Q4 2025 beat recently reported. Industrial real estate is the anti-office play — e-commerce and reshoring provide structural demand tailwinds. Wait for pullback to $82-84 for meaningful upside to analyst targets.
stocks.risks: Q4 beat may already be priced in at ~$87. Rate-sensitive REIT structure. Some European exposure adds FX and geopolitical risk.

stocks.name: TC Energy Corporation
stocks.thesis: Pure-play natural gas infrastructure — 93,000 km of pipelines across Canada, US, Mexico. Spun off South Bow (liquids) in 2024 to focus on nat gas. $3.51/yr dividend, 5.6% yield. Regulated/contracted revenues = steady cashflow. Trump energy policy is a tailwind for continental nat gas. Goldman Sachs raised target to $53 USD ($75 CAD) Feb 18. Analyst consensus target CAD$82. DO NOT BUY AT ATH — wait for pullback to $75-78 CAD where yield opens to ~4.5%+ and upside reappears.
stocks.risks: Payout ratio 106% — dividend slightly stretched vs free cash flow. Debt/equity 2.23 — highly levered. Currently at ALL-TIME HIGH ($86.97 on Feb 13) and above analyst consensus. Buying here = chasing. Keystone XL history shows regulatory/political risk. Wait for the entry.

stocks.name: iShares Core S&P/TSX Capped Composite Index ETF
stocks.thesis: Broadest TSX index exposure — ~240 Canadian stocks, heavily weighted to financials and energy. MER 0.05% (cheapest broad Canadian index ETF). The go-to vehicle for passive TSX exposure in a TFSA. TSX composite just closed at an ALL-TIME HIGH of 33,818 on Feb 21, 2026 — XIC is near ATH at $54. Best entry below $46 (~15% correction). Long-term: Canadian equities are cheap vs S&P 500 on P/E, commodity-backed, and benefit from a weaker CAD. Buy at the right price.
stocks.risks: At ATH — buying now means buying at peak. Heavy financials + energy exposure = Canada-specific concentration risk. USD/CAD moves affect international comparisons. Low yield (2.3%) vs picking individual dividend stocks.

stocks.name: iShares S&P/TSX 60 Index ETF
stocks.thesis: Canada's most liquid ETF — tracks the S&P/TSX 60 (top 60 TSX companies). $21B in assets. More concentrated than XIC but very tradeable. Same core thesis as XIC: excellent long-term hold, terrible entry at ATH. MER 0.15% (3x higher than XIC — prefer XIC for buy-and-hold). Ex-div was today Feb 24 ($0.277/unit). Best entry below $42.
stocks.risks: More concentrated than XIC (60 vs 240 stocks). Higher MER than XIC. At 52-week high — near ATH. XIC is generally superior for long-term buy-and-hold.

stocks.name: Suncor Energy Inc.
stocks.thesis: Canada's premier integrated oil sands company — upstream production, downstream refining, and Petro-Canada retail. Warren Buffett owned it (Berkshire). Aggressive buyback program (billions annually). Analyst avg target ~$48.86 — currently slightly above. Strong FCF generator at $70+ WTI. Wait for pullback below $42-44 to get meaningful upside + 3.5%+ yield.
stocks.risks: Currently trading above analyst consensus target. Oil price sensitive — WTI below $60 compresses margins hard. Oilsands have high breakeven vs shale. Buybacks slow when oil prices drop.

stocks.name: Pembina Pipeline Corporation
stocks.thesis: Western Canadian midstream infrastructure — 18,000 km of pipelines, NGL fractionation, gas processing. Monthly dividends (5.25% yield). Take-or-pay contracts = highly predictable cashflow. One of the most reliable dividend payers on the TSX. Analyst target $58-60 — currently AT the ceiling. Wait for pullback to $52-54 where yield opens to 6%+ and upside returns.
stocks.risks: At analyst consensus ceiling right now. Monthly dividends are attractive but yield compression likely near ATH. Commodity-adjacent despite fee-based model. Acquisition of Alliance/Aux Sable added leverage.

stocks.name: Cenovus Energy Inc.
stocks.thesis: Integrated oil sands + refining + US downstream (acquired Husky). Second-largest Canadian oil company. RBC raised target to C$32 (Outperform) Feb 20. Analyst avg target ~$29-31 CAD. Was at $14.48 eight months ago — has nearly doubled. Variable dividend component means payout grows with oil prices. Best entry below $24 where upside to $32 target = 33%+ plus yield.
stocks.risks: At analyst consensus now. Low base yield (2.5%). Took on significant debt with Husky acquisition — balance sheet still healing. Oil price dependent. Refining margins volatile.

stocks.name: Dollarama Inc.
stocks.thesis: Canada's dominant discount retailer — 1,500+ stores, recession-proof model (foot traffic INCREASES in downturns), 32% EBITDA margins, Q3 revenue +22.4% YoY. Expanding internationally via Dollarcity (Latin America). Analyst avg target C$211-216 = ~10-12% upside from current $192. High estimate C$235. Payout ratio only 9.5% — almost all earnings reinvested. NOT a dividend play (0.21% yield). Pure total return/growth story. Best entry below $170-175 (~15-20% discount to current, meaningful upside to targets).
stocks.risks: Effectively zero dividend — not for income investors. Premium valuation (fwd P/E ~30x). Tariff risk on imported goods (most products sourced from China). FX exposure on Dollarcity earnings. Consumer spending slowdown could compress same-store sales growth.

stocks.name: Celestica Inc.
stocks.thesis: Toronto-based contract manufacturer building AI server hardware for hyperscalers (think: the company that physically assembles the AI infrastructure). Just raised 2026 revenue guidance to $17B. Stock +177% in 12 months. Analyst avg target CAD$571 = ~35% upside. The most direct Canadian pure-play on AI hardware buildout. No dividend — pure growth. Best entry below $350 CAD on a meaningful pullback.
stocks.risks: Already ran 177% in 12 months — buying here means chasing. Low margins (contract manufacturing). Customer concentration risk — loses a hyperscaler contract = big hit. No dividend.

stocks.name: Cameco Corporation
stocks.thesis: World's largest publicly traded uranium company. The AI power thesis: data centres need massive, reliable electricity — nuclear is the only 24/7 clean baseload solution. Microsoft, Amazon, Google all signing nuclear PPAs. Cameco supplies the uranium that fuels those reactors. RBC target C$160 (Outperform). Analyst consensus ~C$172. Stock +128% in 52 weeks. Trades at 90x PE — expensive. Best entry below C$130 where valuation becomes more palatable.
stocks.risks: 90x PE — very expensive on current earnings. Uranium price is volatile. Already +128% in 52 weeks — massive run. Small dividend (0.2%). Nuclear regulatory timelines are long — AI power demand is real but nuclear buildout takes years.

stocks.name: Brookfield Renewable Partners L.P.
stocks.thesis: Brookfield's renewable energy arm — hydro, wind, solar, storage across 30 countries. The AI power angle: tech giants (Microsoft, Google, Meta) are signing 15-20 year PPAs directly with renewable providers to power data centres. BEP is already signing these deals. $10B invested over 5 years for AI-linked power projects. 5% distribution yield + AI demand tailwind = rare combo of income + growth. Best entry below $38-40 CAD.
stocks.risks: Distribution in USD (currency risk for CAD investors). Interest rate sensitive (utilities trade inversely with rates). Complex LP structure has tax implications. Slower growth than pure tech AI plays.

stocks.name: Ivanhoe Mines Ltd.
stocks.thesis: World-class copper producer — Kamoa-Kakula in the DRC is one of the largest and highest-grade copper mines on the planet. AI data centres need massive amounts of copper for wiring, cooling systems, power distribution. EV + AI = structural copper demand supercycle. Stock is DOWN 16% from its 52-week high of $20.34 (hit Jan 29) — the most interesting entry point on this list right now. No dividend yet but FCF building fast.
stocks.risks: No dividend. DRC (Democratic Republic of Congo) = political/operational risk. Copper price volatile. No dividend — capital appreciation only. Robert Friedland-led company (controversial but effective). Currency/sovereign risk in Africa.

stocks.name: Teck Resources Ltd. (Class B)
stocks.thesis: Canada's pure-play copper company after selling its coal assets in 2023. QB2 mine in Chile ramping up = production growth for years. Goldman Sachs and BMO avg target ~C$62 = 13% upside from current. Tops profit estimates on higher copper prices Feb 2026. Structural copper demand from AI infrastructure, EVs, grid buildout. Best entry below $48-50 where upside to $62-75 targets = 25-50% gain.
stocks.risks: QB2 mine in Chile has had engineering challenges (CEO acknowledged Feb 2026). Copper price risk. Chile = higher political/regulatory risk than Canada. Small dividend (1%). Not a significant income play.

stocks.name: Lundin Mining Corporation
stocks.thesis: Diversified base metals miner (copper, zinc, nickel, gold) — Vancouver-based. TD Securities just raised price target to C$45 on Feb 24, 2026. Has run from a 52-week low of C$8.94 to $40 — one of the most explosive moves on the TSX. Not a dividend play (0.3%) but copper demand tailwinds are real. Wait for pullback below $32-35 for a better risk/reward entry.
stocks.risks: Massive 52-week run (+340% from lows) — buying near ATH is high risk. Almost no dividend. Diversified metals = less pure copper exposure than IVN/TECK. Operational risk across multiple jurisdictions.

stocks.name: Hudbay Minerals Inc.
stocks.thesis: Canadian copper + gold + silver + zinc miner — operations in Canada, Peru, and Arizona (Copper World). Strong Buy consensus, analyst target C$31.66. Hit ATH of C$38.80 on Jan 29 (copper all-time high day), has pulled back since. Growing production profile with Copper World development. More diversified than a pure copper play. Best entry below C$24-26 where upside to targets is meaningful.
stocks.risks: Pulled back significantly from ATH — confirm current price before buying. Multi-jurisdictional (Canada, Peru, US) = varied political risk. Copper World (Arizona) project still in permitting. Small dividend.

stocks.name: Emera Inc.
stocks.thesis: Record 2025 EPS of $3.49 (+19% YoY) and first $1B+ adjusted net income milestone. Extended 5-7% EPS growth target through 2030 on Feb 23 is the key catalyst. Regulated Florida (Tampa Electric) + Atlantic Canada utility with zero commodity/tariff exposure. At $65-67 entry, yield opens to 4.4-4.5% with ~8% upside to analyst consensus $71.72 and TD target $75.
stocks.risks: Trading near 52-week high — limited margin of safety at current price. Heavy capex ($3.6B/yr) requires ongoing debt financing; any rate spike hits cost of capital. Nova Scotia Power regulatory headwinds remain a drag.

stocks.name: Manulife Financial Corporation
stocks.thesis: Canada's largest life insurer with top-5 Asia wealth management franchise — the structural growth driver. 55% payout ratio means dividend is well-covered with room to grow. 10+ consecutive years of dividend increases, Canadian eligible dividends. Pulled back ~4% from 52-week high of $52.97 — better entry than 3 months ago. Insurance earnings are structural beneficiaries of higher-for-longer rates.
stocks.risks: Asia operations (Hong Kong, Singapore, Mainland China) introduce geopolitical and FX risk. Insurance earnings can be lumpy. Not a pure boring utility — actuarial surprises happen.

stocks.name: Empire Company Ltd. (Sobeys)
stocks.thesis: Sobeys, FreshCo, IGA and Farm Boy operator — Atlantic and Western Canada grocery dominance. Trades at ~12x P/E vs Metro/Loblaw peers at ~18x, a meaningful valuation discount. Groceries are structurally recession-proof with pricing power. Scene+ loyalty program is an underappreciated margin catalyst. Best entry below $33 where yield approaches 3% and upside to fair value is 15-20%.
stocks.risks: Grocery margin compression from private label competition and discounters. Western Canada concentration creates regional economic exposure. Loblaw price investment cycle can compress sector multiples.

stocks.name: The Toronto-Dominion Bank
stocks.thesis: Record Q1 2026 EPS of C$2.44 (+8% beat vs C$2.26 consensus), net income +45% YoY to C$4.04B. Core Canadian banking is firing. But at ATH ($134.57 CAD = ALL-TIME HIGH), TD trades 4.2% ABOVE analyst consensus target of C$129.17 — not a great entry. US asset cap ($434B, imposed post-$3.09B AML penalty) remains the overhang: until the Fed lifts it (2026-2028?), US retail growth is frozen. New CEO Raymond Chun executing cleanly. Best entry at $118-122 CAD (3.5-3.7% yield) on any pullback.
stocks.risks: US asset cap is an indefinite headwind on the highest-growth segment. Currently AT all-time high ($134.69) and above analyst consensus — zero margin of safety at current price. AML remediation costs continue (full resolution timeline unclear). Buying ATH after an earnings pop is chasing.

stocks.name: Keyera Corp.
stocks.thesis: Keyera closed the transformative $5.15B Plains Midstream Canada NGL acquisition in Q1 2026, making it one of Canada's largest NGL infrastructure operators. Record 2025 EBITDA of $1.16B, DCF of $3.36/share covers the $2.16 annual dividend 1.55x. Management guides 7-8% EBITDA growth for 2026. Analyst consensus of $51.09 is pre-Plains modelling — upgrades to $54-65 range are coming as buy-side models in the acquired cashflows. ~80% fee-based/take-or-pay revenues = toll-road model, not an oil price bet. Best entry $47-48 (4.5%+ yield, 15-30% upside to post-acquisition targets).
stocks.risks: Integration risk on $5.15B Plains deal. Marketing margin volatile ($300M in 2025 vs $485M prior year, guidance withheld until May 2026). Elevated leverage post-deal limits M&A flexibility. Stock at analyst consensus now — needs upgrade cycle to move higher.

stocks.name: Exchange Income Corporation
stocks.thesis: Diversified industrial conglomerate (aerospace/aviation + manufacturing) with monthly distributions. Q4 2025 earnings beat (Feb 25, 2026) triggered four analyst upgrades in one day: TD $125, Raymond James $125, Ventum $135, consensus repriced to $114.42. Stock broke to new 52-week highs — 5-24% upside remains to analyst targets. Not a high-yield play (2.5%) — total return story with strong management track record. Watch for post-earnings-pop consolidation to $104-106 for better risk/reward entry.
stocks.risks: Post-earnings pop means buying at new highs. Moderate yield (2.5%) limits income appeal. Aviation segment cyclically exposed. Significant debt load ($3.5B credit facility).

stocks.name: Algonquin Power & Utilities Corp.
stocks.thesis: Pivoted to pure regulated utility after selling Liberty Renewables. Now focused on regulated water, gas, and electric distribution with aggressive debt reduction. Q4 2025 earnings due March 6, 2026 — first clean quarter as simplified utility. Targets 20% EPS growth in 2026. Analyst Buy consensus, targets C$10-11. Near 52-week high; if management delivers on deleverage and earnings growth, this is a boring regulated utility in recovery mode and a potential 2026-2027 dividend rebuilder.
stocks.risks: Dividend was cut — not for income today. Near 52-week high after major recovery from $6.17 low. March 6 earnings are make-or-break for the deleverage thesis. Speculative — size accordingly.

stocks.name: Brookfield Infrastructure Partners L.P.
stocks.thesis: Global infrastructure LP (utilities, transport, midstream, data) with 17 consecutive annual distribution increases. Q4 2025 FFO $3.32/unit (+6% YoY), EPS $0.87 vs $0.34 estimate — massive beat. Distribution raised 6% (17th straight year). 1.93x FFO coverage ratio means zero cut risk. Data segment +50% YoY (hidden AI infrastructure play). 2026 FFO guidance: +10%+ per unit. Trades at ~10.3x 2026E FFO vs infrastructure peer average of 11-14x — re-rating to 12x implies $62.57 CAD (+16.6% upside) plus 4.6% distribution yield = 21%+ total return on bull case. Hold in RRSP.
stocks.risks: USD distributions expose CAD investors to FX risk. Rate-sensitive — BoC/Fed tightening compresses infrastructure multiples. Near 52-week high with limited price cushion. LP tax complexity — RRSP/TFSA recommended. Best entry $50-51 CAD.

stocks.name: Hydro One Limited
stocks.thesis: Ontario's monopoly electricity transmission grid — 47% government-owned, cannot be disrupted or taken over. Q4 2025 EPS +18% YoY; full-year EPS $2.23 (+15.5%). OEB-regulated rate base growth locks in 6-8% annual EPS growth through 2027. Ontario electrification (EVs, AI data centres, industrial reshoring) is a structural demand tailwind. Buy at $51-53 CAD where yield opens to 2.5%+ and P/E drops to ~23x — analyst consensus zone.
stocks.risks: Currently 9% above analyst consensus target of $53.40 — zero margin of safety at $58. BoC rate pause/reversal hits regulated utilities hard. Low 2.27% yield at current price is unattractive vs pipeline/REIT alternatives. OEB can cut allowed ROE.

stocks.name: Metro Inc.
stocks.thesis: Canada's third-largest grocer with 30 consecutive years of dividend increases, just hiking the payout 10.1% in Q1 FY2026 despite a temporary distribution centre shutdown that caused an earnings-day selloff. Grocery oligopoly with real pricing power, tariff-proof domestic business, beta 0.34. BMO target  = 17.5% upside. Best entry -97 CAD.
stocks.risks: Low current yield (1.67%) — a dividend growth story, not income. Distribution centre drag could compress Q2 margins and re-test  if the rebuild takes longer than expected.

stocks.name: Sun Life Financial Inc.
stocks.thesis: Canada's second-largest insurer with a growing Asia wealth management franchise as its structural growth engine. Structural beneficiary of higher-for-longer rates. Well-covered dividend (55% payout), 10+ consecutive years of raises. Next payment March 31, 2026. Canadian eligible dividends.
stocks.risks: Asia operations (Hong Kong, Singapore, China) introduce geopolitical and FX risk. Insurance earnings can be lumpy. Near 52-week highs — wait for entry below $93 CAD for 4%+ yield.

stocks.name: Canadian National Railway Company
stocks.thesis: The most irreplaceable physical infrastructure in Canada — one of two Class 1 railways connecting Canada coast-to-coast and into the US Midwest. 29 consecutive years of dividend raises ($0.92/quarter = $3.68/yr). Toll-road model: shippers have no alternative. Buy on first 10-15% broad market correction.
stocks.risks: Near fair value currently — no margin of safety at current price. Rail volumes cyclically sensitive to industrial activity. Two-player oligopoly means CP Rail is the only competitor for volume share.

stocks.name: Alimentation Couche-Tard Inc.
stocks.thesis: World class convenience store operator (17,000+ stores) trading at 21.5x P/E — 9% below its 10-year historical average PE of 23.49x. On Feb 11, 2026, management issued first-ever formal guidance: 10%+ adjusted EPS CAGR to FY2030, >US$2.5B FY2026 FCF. The 7-Eleven walkaway reset the multiple while earnings kept growing — forward P/E drops to ~15x by FY2030 if guidance holds. 15+ consecutive years of dividend increases, 20% payout ratio, tariff-proof domestic operations.
stocks.risks: ~35% of gross profit from road transportation fuel creates long-term EV headwind; stock near 52-week high with no price margin of safety — wait for dip to $79-80 for best entry.

stocks.name: Northland Power Inc.
stocks.thesis: Renewable utility trading 27% below 5-year average after a strategic 40% dividend cut to fund offshore wind construction. Q4 2025 EBITDA +25% YoY, 2026 guidance of C$1.45-1.65B implies 16-32% growth. Baltic Power (1.1 GW) on track for H2 2026, Hai Long (1.0 GW) for 2027 — once online, FCF ramps and dividend growth resumes.
stocks.risks: Hai Long commissioning delays could impact C$150-200M pre-completion revenue. Debt/equity 181% — heavy project-finance leverage. Rate-sensitive.

stocks.name: AltaGas Ltd.
stocks.thesis: Hybrid midstream/utility with REEF LPG export terminal (first-mover Canadian LPG exports to Asia-Pacific) coming online 2026, plus regulated US gas distribution. Six consecutive 6% dividend raises, 5-7% CAGR guided through 2030. 2026 EBITDA guidance $1.925-2.025B (+8% YoY). Quality compounder but at ATH — wait for pullback to $42-43 for 3.1%+ yield and 10-15% upside.
stocks.risks: At all-time high with only 4% upside to analyst consensus. Leverage 4.5-5.0x. REEF construction risk. 7% share dilution over 5 years.

stocks.name: Canadian Apartment Properties REIT (CAPREIT)
stocks.thesis: Canada's largest residential REIT trading at a 33% discount to NAV ($56.41/unit) near 52-week lows. 45,900 units of essential housing backed by Canada's structural housing shortage (400K+ immigrants/year). Monthly distributions with ultra-conservative 61% FFO payout ratio. Same-property NOI +4.7% YoY, rents +5%, 97% occupancy. Analyst consensus target C$46.74 = 23% upside plus 4.1% yield.
stocks.risks: Management flagged softer rental market; rate-sensitive REIT; Netherlands exposure being divested but adds near-term complexity; revenue declined on dispositions (per-unit metrics improved).

stocks.name: Saputo Inc.
stocks.thesis: Canada's largest dairy processor with PEG of 0.56 and beta of 0.04 — rare defensive + growth combo. Just broke above 200-day MA after 12+ months below. Five analyst upgrades in Feb 2026. Q3 FY2026 Canada segment posted record profitability. Earnings recovering as dairy commodity costs normalize.
stocks.risks: Near 52-week high with limited margin of safety. 43% US revenue exposure creates tariff risk. Dairy commodity swings can compress margins quarter-to-quarter.

stocks.name: SmartCentres Real Estate Investment Trust
stocks.thesis: Canada's largest Walmart-anchored REIT trading at a 27% discount to NAV (C$35.65/unit). 98.6% occupancy, 84% AFFO payout ratio, monthly distributions never cut through COVID. Rate cuts are a direct tailwind for NAV re-rating. Buying essential real estate at 73 cents on the dollar.
stocks.risks: Rate-sensitive REIT; real thesis is NAV gap closing over 2-3 years, near-term analyst upside limited to 6.7%. Development income lumpy.

stocks.name: Capital Power Corporation
stocks.thesis: North American power generator (6,400+ MW) with structural AI data centre electricity demand tailwind. PEG of 0.91 means growth is underpriced. 10 analysts, Moderate Buy consensus, avg target C$76.56 = 20% upside + 4.3% yield. Earnings March 4, 2026 is near-term catalyst. Best entry C$55-58 where yield opens to 4.8-5.0%.
stocks.risks: P/E of 23x elevated for a power generator; 136% debt-to-equity; Alberta power market volatility; coal-to-gas transition execution risk.

stocks.name: Rogers Communications Inc.
stocks.thesis: Canadian telecom oligopoly trading at 10.84x PE — a 30% discount to its 5-year average of 15-16x. Shaw acquisition synergies materializing: FY2025 revenue C$21.71B (+5.4%), earnings +297%. Still 30% below 2022 ATH despite being the largest wireless carrier post-Shaw (12.1M subs). Deleveraging catalyst as C$40B acquisition debt gets paid down.
stocks.risks: Earnings normalization (297% jump includes one-time Shaw integration benefits — normalized PE likely 12-14x). C$40B+ debt load. CRTC regulatory risk. Quebecor/Freedom Mobile emerging as 4th national carrier.

stocks.name: Great-West Lifeco Inc.
stocks.thesis: Record 2025 base earnings ($4.6B, +11%), 10% dividend hike to C$0.67/qtr, base ROE 18.2% targeting 19%+. Empower (#2 US retirement platform) driving double-digit growth. Forward P/E ~13.1x on $5.03 base EPS is cheap for a financial delivering 18% ROE. $3.3T client assets, $1.6B buybacks in 2025. Boring, diversified, growing.
stocks.risks: Near 52-week high with limited margin of safety. Empower growth tied to US equity market fees. Insurance actuarial surprises possible. Best entry C$62-64.

stocks.name: Power Corporation of Canada
stocks.thesis: Century-old holding company (Great-West Lifeco, IGM Financial, GBL) at 14.1x P/E — 22% discount to TSX avg. GWL just posted RECORD 2025 base earnings ($4.6B, +11%) and raised dividend 10%. NAV discount narrowed from 24% to 12% — the 10% pullback from Dec highs re-opens the discount as a re-entry window. 51.5% payout ratio means dividend growth ahead.
stocks.risks: Holding company discount can persist. Complex structure (GWO + IGM + GBL). European exposure via GBL adds FX/geopolitical risk. Already tripled from 2021 lows.

stocks.name: Killam Apartment REIT
stocks.thesis: Atlantic Canada residential REIT trading 25% below 2021 levels at 13.9x P/FFO (peers 16-20x). Housing crisis + immigration = structural demand. FFO/unit grew 4.2% YoY, AFFO payout ratio 69%, 96.9% occupancy. Monthly payer. Rate cuts are a direct tailwind for REIT re-rating. Analyst consensus C$21.30 = 24% upside.
stocks.risks: Rate-sensitive REIT; Atlantic Canada concentration; provincial rent control risk; C$120.5M fair value losses in 2025.

stocks.name: Canadian Tire Corporation
stocks.thesis: Iconic Canadian retailer trading at 13.47x P/E — a 10% discount to its 10-year average of 14.91x — despite delivering 19% EPS growth in FY2025. PEG of 0.35 means growth is massively underpriced. 100% domestic revenue = zero tariff exposure, making it a rare safe haven. Three businesses in one: retail (Canadian Tire, SportChek, Mark's), CT REIT, and Financial Services (Triangle Rewards 16M+ members).
stocks.risks: Near 52-week high with limited short-term margin of safety. D/E of 173% elevated (includes Financial Services lending). FY2025 benefited from extra retail week. Best entry on pullback to C$175-180.

stocks.name: Russel Metals Inc.
stocks.thesis: Industrial metals distributor with PEG of 0.59 — growth massively underpriced at 16x P/E. Kloeckner acquisition closed Dec 31, 2025 transforms the business; analyst models still catching up. FY2025 revenue C$4.6B, 58% payout ratio, $86M buybacks. Value-added processing pivot = margin expansion story.
stocks.risks: Cyclical construction/energy exposure; Kloeckner integration risk; tariff uncertainty on steel volumes and pricing.

stocks.name: CT REIT
stocks.thesis: Canadian Tire's real estate arm — own the buildings CTC operates from. Monthly distributions, grocery-anchored stability. Near 52-week high after analyst upgrade. Wait for pullback to C$15.50-16 for 6%+ yield entry. Perfect TFSA income vehicle.
stocks.risks: Near 52-week high with limited upside to analyst consensus. Rate-sensitive REIT. Concentrated tenant exposure to Canadian Tire.

stocks.name: Mullen Group Ltd.
stocks.thesis: Western Canadian trucking and logistics — boring backbone of Canadian supply chains. Monthly C$0.07 distributions (C$0.84/yr). Oil & gas services + LTL freight. Canadian eligible dividends. Buy-and-forget 5% income play.
stocks.risks: Western Canada energy exposure creates cyclicality. Trucking is competitive with thin margins. Volume-sensitive to economic slowdowns.

stocks.name: Dream Industrial REIT
stocks.thesis: Industrial REIT trading below 5-year average after rate-hike drawdown — still 22% below 2021 highs. Rate-cut cycle is the direct catalyst for NAV re-rating. Three analyst upgrades in 10 days (Desjardins, Raymond James, RBC). Monthly distributions, geographic diversification (Canada/US/Europe), and structural e-commerce/reshoring warehouse demand.
stocks.risks: Rate-sensitive REIT; European FX exposure; near 52-week high — better entry on pullback to C$12.50.

stocks.name: Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce
stocks.thesis: Last Big 5 bank not on the watchlist. Record Q1 2026 revenue just reported Feb 25. P/E 15.7x reasonable for Canadian bank oligopoly. Canadian eligible dividends. Near 52-week highs — wait for pullback to C$125 for 3.4%+ yield entry.
stocks.risks: Near 52-week high with limited margin of safety. US commercial real estate loan book is a potential headwind.

stocks.name: Gibson Energy Inc.
stocks.thesis: Boring midstream oil infrastructure (terminals, pipelines). Record Q4 infra EBITDA of $160M. 7th consecutive 5% dividend increase. $400M Teine acquisition expands Canadian crude footprint. Targets 7%+ infra EBITDA/share growth over 5 years. BUT at new 52-week high and above analyst consensus of C$27.50.
stocks.risks: At ATH and above analyst consensus — no margin of safety at current price. 84% payout ratio. D/E 278%. Wait for pullback.

stocks.name: Quebecor Inc.
stocks.thesis: Canada's 4th national carrier trading at 6.5x EV/EBITDA — a 25-35% discount to telecom peers (BCE 7-8x, Rogers 7.5x, TELUS 8x) despite having the BEST EBITDA margins in the sector at 40%. Freedom Mobile acquisition (1M+ subs in ON/BC/AB) transforms Quebecor from a Quebec regional player to a national telecom. ROE of 37.4% is absurd for a telecom. Six analyst upgrades on Feb 28 alone. 100% Canadian domestic revenue = tariff-proof.
stocks.risks: Debt/equity 364% from Freedom Mobile acquisition. Near 52-week high with limited short-term margin of safety. Freedom Mobile integration still maturing in new markets. Quebec concentration remains ~60% of revenue.

stocks.name: Intact Financial Corporation
stocks.thesis: Canada's #1 P&C insurer at 14.1x P/E with 19.5% ROE — record FY2025 NOIPS of C$19.21 (+33%) and 48% EPS growth. Stock 17% below 52-week high after post-earnings overreaction despite beating estimates. 32% payout ratio with 10-year dividend CAGR of 10% (21 consecutive increases) means the dividend doubles in ~7 years. Ex-div March 17 at new higher C$1.47/qtr (11% hike). C$3.7B capital margin for acquisitions.
stocks.risks: UK&I segment underperforming (-2% DPW). P/E of 14.1x above insurance peer average of 12.8x. Low yield (2.25%) — total return play, not high income. Catastrophe losses can spike unpredictably.

stocks.name: North West Company Inc.
stocks.thesis: Canada's remote grocery monopoly — operates stores in northern communities with zero competition, revenue backstopped by government Nutrition North subsidies. Just added to TSX Composite Index forcing passive fund buying. Next 100 initiative driving EBITDA +8% and EPS +14% YoY. Cheaper than Loblaw/Metro at 18.8x P/E despite stronger moat.
stocks.risks: Near 52-week high with limited margin of safety. Northern logistics exposure to weather disruption. Low liquidity (~138K avg volume). Government subsidy policy changes could impact demand.

stocks.name: Stella-Jones Inc.
stocks.thesis: North America's dominant utility pole and railway tie manufacturer with 22 consecutive dividend increases and a 22% payout ratio — massive room for dividend growth. Q4 2025 miss (EPS $0.91 vs $1.02 est) created an 8% pullback from $101 high, but full-year EBITDA of $661M at 18.9% margin exceeded targets. Negative beta (-0.17) makes it a rare portfolio hedge. Utility pole demand recovering strongly (+9% Q4 organic volume growth).
stocks.risks: Railway ties demand softening (-16% Q4 YoY). Residential lumber exposure to housing cycle. Not cheap on pure price history — 10% above 1-year average.

stocks.name: National Bank of Canada
stocks.thesis: CWB acquisition transforms NA from Quebec's bank to pan-Canadian 6th largest. Q1 2026: net income +26%, P&C +47%, commercial lending +54%. Flawless integration. BUT at C$186 trading at 95% of 52-week range with P/E of 17.9x vs 10-year avg 10.5x — fully priced. Great company, bad price. Wait for pullback to C$155-165 where yield opens to 3.0-3.2% and P/E normalizes to ~15x.
stocks.risks: Overvalued at ATH — 71% above 10-year average P/E, only 2.6% below analyst consensus target. CWB integration costs still flowing. Quebec-heavy revenue base. Canadian economic slowdown compresses P&C loan growth.

stocks.name: Nutrien Ltd.
stocks.thesis: World's largest fertilizer company (Saskatoon HQ) — potash, nitrogen, phosphate + 2,000 retail locations. Record 2025 results: EPS US$4.66 (+243%), FCF US$2.15B (+45%), 27.5M tonnes record sales. BUT near 52-week high with DOJ antitrust investigation (Mar 4, 2026) and Belarus sanctions removal creating headwinds. Buy at C$85-90 where yield opens to 3.4%+ and you're at the 5-year average.
stocks.risks: DOJ antitrust probe could escalate. Belarus potash supply returning. Most analyst targets below current price. Near 52-week high with no margin of safety.

stocks.name: Bank of Montreal
stocks.thesis: Canada's 4th largest bank has finally turned the corner on its US$16.3B Bank of the West acquisition. Record Q1 2026 earnings beat estimates (adj. EPS C$3.48 vs C$3.20 est, +15% YoY), with a 5% dividend hike to C$1.67/quarter and 6M-share buyback signaling management confidence. At ~14x forward earnings, BMO trades cheaper than peers RBC (16.5x) and TD (15.2x) despite having the strongest near-term earnings growth trajectory. Simply Wall St estimates 28.8% below intrinsic value. The 'new BMO' with integrated US operations is structurally more profitable — this is not a reversion-to-mean trade, it's a catch-up trade.
stocks.risks: Stock near 52-week high limits margin of safety. US tariff uncertainty could impact cross-border operations. Bank of the West credit portfolio may have hidden quality issues as US rates bite. P/E of 14x is reasonable but not cheap vs historical bank multiples of 10-12x.

stocks.name: Russel Metals Inc.
stocks.thesis: Boring industrial steel distributor growing via smart acquisitions. Q4 2025: $4.6B revenue (+9% YoY), EBITDA $337M (+13%), record 1.6M tons shipped. Closed Kloeckner acquisition (7 US service centers, US$95M) Dec 31. Net debt/invested capital only 10%, $515M liquidity. ROE averaged 18% over 3 years. Canadian eligible dividend.
stocks.risks: Steel is cyclical — trade tensions/tariffs could impact margins. Q4 EBITDA dipped QoQ ($69M vs $75M Q3). Kloeckner integration execution risk.

stocks.name: IGM Financial Inc.
stocks.thesis: Canada's largest independent wealth manager (IG Wealth, Mackenzie) with record AUM&A of $326.6B (+17.4% YoY) and record adjusted EPS of C$4.64. 10% dividend hike to $2.48/yr with 53% payout ratio. Hidden value: Wealthsimple ($2.3B), Rockefeller Capital ($1.16B), ChinaAMC, Northleaf — $3.5B+ strategic holdings = ~23% of market cap. $1B unallocated capital. At 14x earnings with embedded fintech upside, the core business is cheaper than it looks.
stocks.risks: Above all historical price averages — business transformation justifies premium but no margin of safety at ATH. AUM fees tied to equity markets. Power Corp controls voting shares. Beta 1.11.

stocks.name: Chartwell Retirement Residences
stocks.thesis: Canada's largest seniors housing REIT with 30,000+ suites, riding a 15-year demographic supercycle as baby boomers hit 85+. Q4 2025 FFO grew 40.9% YoY, occupancy hit 95.2%, and new supply is at multi-decade lows. Monthly payer with 64% FFO payout ratio — substantial distribution growth ahead.
stocks.risks: Leveraged (D/E 176%) for acquisitions. Rate-sensitive REIT above all historical price averages. Labour cost inflation in healthcare/PSW staff remains a headwind.

stocks.name: iA Financial Corporation Inc.
stocks.thesis: Quebec-based insurer (est. 1892) trading 18% below 52-week high after 11% post-earnings overreaction on Feb 18 despite record FY2025 results. Director bought C$912K at C$152 on Feb 19 — massive insider conviction. Core EPS C$12.96 (+16% YoY), core ROE 17.1%, payout ratio just 31.9%. Dividend doubled in 5 years and just raised 10%. At 11.5x core earnings, cheapest CDN insurer with best growth profile.
stocks.risks: Low yield (2.65%) — dividend growth not income. Analysts mostly Hold. Insurance earnings can be lumpy. Near 1-year average price, not a screaming bottom.
